classes.reasoning: Document explicitly titled 'INFORMED CONSENT FORM' with signature blocks for participant and investigator consent, structured consent checklist ('I have read and understood this form', 'I agree to take part in this study'), and typical ICF sections covering purpose, risks, benefits, voluntary participation, and withdrawal rights.

trial_title: A Phase II Study of BRC-471 (Lumitanib) in Advanced Lung Cancer
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC that has progressed on osimertinib
summary: This is version 2.1 of the Informed Consent Form for study BIORCE-ONC-2023-001, a Phase II trial testing BRC-471 (Lumitanib) in patients with advanced EGFR-mutant non-small cell lung cancer progressing after osimertinib. The document explains the study purpose, procedures, risks, benefits, and patient rights, and includes signature blocks for participant and investigator consent.
content:
BIORCE 
THERAPEUTICS


Study: BIORCE-ONC-2023-001  |  ICF v2.1  |  Date: 09 November 2023

  EudraCT: 2023-001234-11  |  Supersedes ICF v2.0 (22 August 2023)




INFORMED CONSENT FORM


A Phase II Study of BRC-471 (Lumitanib) in Advanced Lung Cancer


IMPORTANT:
 Please read this document carefully. You may take it home.
Ask any questions before signing. Participation is completely voluntary.


1. Introduction


You are being asked to take part in a clinical research study testing BRC-471 (Lumitanib),
an experimental medicine, in patients with EGFR-mutant NSCLC that has progressed on osimertinib.


2. Purpose of This Study


We want to find out whether BRC-471 can shrink or stop your cancer, how long any benefit lasts,
what side effects it causes, and how your body processes it.


3. Duration


You will take BRC-471 until your cancer grows or side effects are unmanageable.
Most patients remain on treatment for several months to over a year.
Follow-up visits every 12 weeks for at least one year after stopping.


4. What Will Happen


Visit
What happens


Screening (up to 4 weeks)
Blood tests, CT scan, ECG, physical exam, tumour biopsy if needed


Treatment cycles (28 days each)
BRC-471 tablets twice daily; blood tests; CT every 8 weeks


End of Treatment
Full assessment — scans, blood tests, questionnaires


Follow-up
Visits or calls every 12 weeks


You will take 
2 tablets of BRC-471 twice a day
. Also: ondansetron (anti-nausea)
during the first week of each cycle. Do not crush or chew tablets.


5. Possible Risks


Side Effect
How Common
Usually Mild?


Diarrhoea
~7 in 10 patients
Usually mild


Skin rash
~6 in 10 patients
Usually mild-moderate


Nausea
~5 in 10 patients
Anti-nausea medicine given


Fatigue
~5 in 10 patients
Usually mild


Lung inflammation
~1 in 25 patients
Can be serious — contact team if breathless


6. Possible Benefits


You may benefit if BRC-471 shrinks or slows your cancer. This cannot be guaranteed.


7. Your Right to Withdraw


Participation is entirely voluntary.
 You may withdraw at any time
without penalty or effect on your standard medical care.


8. Privacy


Data processed per EU GDPR. Study documents use a participant code, not your name.


9. Costs


No cost to you. Medicine provided free. Reasonable travel expenses reimbursed.


10. Contact


For...
Contact


Study / health questions
Your study doctor (details on file)


Your rights
Local Ethics Committee (site-provided)


Urgent safety (24/7)
+34 900 123 456




11. Consent


Initial each statement, then sign below.


I have read and understood this form (v2.1, 09 November 2023).


I have had the opportunity to ask questions and all have been answered.


I understand participation is voluntary and I may withdraw at any time.


I agree to collection and processing of my personal data.


I agree to take part in this study.




Participant Full Name (PRINT)


Participant Signature


Date (DD/MM/YYYY)


Investigator Full Name (PRINT)


Investigator Signature


Date (DD/MM/YYYY)




A signed copy will be given to you.




BIORCE-ONC-2023-001 ICF v2.1 · CONFIDENTIAL · © 2023 Biorce Therapeutics S.L.
